supportingFeatures: Unexpected panic episodes with rapid physical escalation
Avoidance and safety behaviors tied to fear of recurrence
ruleOuts: Generalized Anxiety Disorder because the fear is attack-focused rather than diffuse across domains
Social Anxiety Disorder because episodes are not limited to evaluation situations
Adjustment Disorder with Anxiety because the symptom pattern shows panic-specific recurrence and avoidance

supportingFeatures: Associated restlessness, sleep disturbance, fatigue, and muscle tension
ruleOuts: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder because the presentation lacks intrusive obsessions with compulsive neutralizing rituals
Panic Disorder because there are no recurrent unexpected panic attacks as the core feature
Persistent Depressive Disorder because worry and physiological tension are more central than chronic low mood

supportingFeatures: Avoidance of presentations, meetings, and eating in public
Functional impairment in academic and work roles
ruleOuts: Avoidant Personality Disorder because the available data centers on circumscribed anxiety symptoms rather than a pervasive lifelong personality pattern
Panic Disorder because the anxiety is linked to social scrutiny contexts
Autism Spectrum Disorder because the concern is embarrassment and evaluation, not a primary social communication difference

supportingFeatures: Avoidance and emotional numbing persisting for months
ruleOuts: Acute Stress Disorder because the symptoms have persisted well beyond the acute post-trauma window
Adjustment Disorder because the full trauma-related symptom cluster is present
Panic Disorder because the distress is trauma-cued rather than unexpected recurring panic

supportingFeatures: Compulsive washing and checking for temporary relief
ruleOuts: Illness Anxiety Disorder because the concern is contamination and ritual neutralization rather than fear of having a disease
Generalized Anxiety Disorder because the pattern is obsession-compulsion driven, not diffuse worry
Specific Phobia because the rituals and intrusive thoughts extend beyond simple avoidance of a discrete object or situation

supportingFeatures: Low mood and loss of interest
Vegetative and cognitive symptoms including appetite, sleep, and concentration changes
Episode duration and impairment without evidence of mania
ruleOuts: Persistent Depressive Disorder because the available timeline reflects an acute major episode rather than chronic multi-year depressed mood
Adjustment Disorder with Depressed Mood because the symptom severity and syndrome pattern exceed a brief adjustment response
Bipolar II Disorder because there is no evidence of hypomanic episodes
